The d+ beam energy was 300 keV and the intensity
was 5 milliAmps. A sample transport system of six
pneumatic tubes at angles of 0, 50, 75, 105, 125 and
155 deg to the deuteron beam provided irradiation
MeV. Another tube was put at -105 deg to check the
symmetry of the system. The irradiation positions were
about 15 cm from the tritium target. An additional
tube set at -22.5 deg and at 1.5 cm distance was used
when strong activities were required. The transfer
time was about 2 sec. The effective reaction neutron
energy at each position was determined by the ratio of

.The foil samples had dimensions 10 mm x 10 mm and were
between 0.1 - 0.2 mm thick. Powder samples were
wrapped in powder papers and were about 1 mm thick.
.The neutron flux at the irradiation positions was

Two Ge detectors with efficiencies of 12 and
respectively, were used to measure the induced activi-
ties. Each detector was covered with a 5 mm acrylic
absorber to reduce beta-rays. The efficiency calibra-
tion was performed at a distance of 5 cm using 24-Na,
the efficiency curve is estimated to be 5, 3 and 1.5%
in the energy region below 80 keV, 80 - 300 keV
for all reactions in data Subents; based on the
